title: leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. pseudotomentosa
intro: Leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. pseudotomentosa, also known as the alpine daisy, is a flowering plant species found in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. pseudotomentosa include its ability to soothe digestive discomfort, relieve coughs and congestion, and act as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
